text:
Harper sat down and opened her latest novel. She felt something stirring inside her. A mischievous grin spread across her face. She began to make silly voices for her characters. She giggled until tears rolled down her cheeks. All her woes seemed small beside the chaos she was conjuring.

She thought about the monster lurking under their beds. Maybe tonight she'd throw enough garlic cloves around to drive it away. Her laugh echoed through the silence.

Then came the unexpected. Her cat darted into the room. Harper watched him squint at the strange voices. He gave her a doubtful look before jumping onto her bed.

"Well," Harper murmured. "You seem quite content."

She tucked him under her arm. The mischief died. Tomorrow was another day. For now, she would write some more. Maybe she'd include him. After all, he could give a purrfect cat voice.

She closed her eyes. Her characters danced through her mind, leaving trails of sugar trails.
